Caption: Normalized bubble radius (R 1/R 01) of a small bubble (R 01 = 2.5 μm) versus the period number of dual-frequency driving (n) in some representative cases. (a) Single bubble case and (b-e) two interacting bubble cases in which the second bubbles have different initial radii, i.e., (b) R 02 = 3.2 μm, (c) R 02 = 7 μm, (d) R 02 = 8.2 μm and (e) R 02 = 10 μm.

Normalized bubble radius (R 1/R 01) of a large bubble (R 01 = 8.2 μm) versus the period number of dual-frequency driving (n) in some representative cases. (a) Single bubble case and (b-d) two interacting bubble cases where the second bubbles have different initial radii, i.e., (b) R 02 = 3.2 μm, (c) R 02 = 7 μm and (d) R 02 = 10 μm.

(a) Oscillation amplitudes (R max/R 0) and (b, c) bifurcation diagrams of a two-bubble system with R 01 = 2.5 μm as a function of the initial radius of the second bubble (R 02 = 1–10 μm). (d-f) The corresponding results in the case of R 01 = 8.2 μm. The symbols ‘w/o’ and ‘w’ mean the cases without and with the interaction effects between bubbles, respectively.

Oscillation amplitudes of a two-bubble system, in which (a) one bubble with an initial radius of R 01 = 2.5 μm and (b) the other bubble with a varying initial radius (R 02 = 1–10 μm), as a function of R 02 at various initial inter-bubble distances (d 0). (c) Oscillation amplitudes and (d) bifurcation diagrams of a two-bubble system (R 01 = 2.5 μm and R 02 = 8.2 μm) as a function of d 0.

Oscillation amplitudes of a two-bubble system (R 01 = 3.2 μm and R 02 = 8.2 μm) as a function of the frequency f 0 = (f 1 + f 2)/2. A change of the f 0 can obtain different frequency combinations of the two components of dual-frequency ultrasound, in which the first frequency is f 1 = f 0-Δf/2 and the second frequency is f 2 = f 0 + Δf/2. The frequency difference between the two components Δf is set as (a) Δf = 0.2 MHz, (b) Δf = 0.3 MHz and (c) Δf = 0.4 MHz.

Frequency-response curves of two-bubble systems with different ultrasound amplitude ratios between the two components of dual-frequency driving β, including (a) β = 0.1, (b) β = 1 and (c) β = 10. Note that f 0 = (f 1 + f 2)/2, where f 1 = f 0-Δf/2, f 2 = f 0 + Δf/2 and Δf = 0.3 MHz.

Effects of the acoustic pressure amplitude pA on the cavitation dynamics of a two-bubble system (R 01 = 3.2 μm and R 02 = 8.2 μm). (a) Oscillation amplitudes as a function of pA , as well as (b) and (c) the bifurcation diagrams of the normalized radius (R/R 0) of the two-bubble system as a function of pA for the non-interacting (w/o) and interacting (w) cases, respectively.

Oscillation amplitudes of a two-bubble system (i.e., Rmax 1/R 01 and Rmax 2/R 02) as a function of medium elasticity G and medium viscosity μ, including (a) a small bubble (R 01 = 3.2 μm) and (b) a large bubble (R 02 = 8.2 μm). (c) Bifurcation diagram of the normalized bubble radii (R/R 0) for the two bubbles as a function of G at a fixed μ = 1 mPa∙s. The symbol ‘w/o’ means that the interaction effects between bubbles are not considered. When the interaction effects are considered (w), the corresponding results are presented in (d)-(f), respectively.
